cases.prompt: add a ruleset requiring PR reviews
enable secret scanning and push protection
require the CI check to pass before merge
lock down who can merge to main
fix this CodeQL alert in my code
set up OIDC trusted publishing to PyPI
review this PR for security issues

expected_behavior: creates a ruleset (not classic branch protection) via gh api: require PR, required status checks named exactly as they report, block force pushes and deletions
requires code-owner review only if CODEOWNERS exists, and says why (CODEOWNERS is inert otherwise)
verifies the ruleset landed via gh api and shows the user how to bypass-audit it
notes plan limitations (rulesets/protection on private repos need a paid plan) instead of failing silently

cases.prompt: set up dependabot for this repo
expected_behavior: writes .github/dependabot.yml for the ecosystems actually present in the repo, weekly, with grouped updates and a cooldown
explains security updates bypass cooldown by design and cooldown does not cover transitive npm dependencies
recommends against blanket auto-merge of bot PRs (bot PRs need the same CI + review gates)

cases.prompt: we run a CI matrix - which status check do I make required?
expected_behavior: recommends a single aggregator job (needs: matrix job, if: always, fails unless all legs succeeded) as the only required check
warns that requiring individual matrix legs is brittle and that merge queues need the merge_group trigger
shows how to find the exact check name GitHub reports

cases.prompt: enable all the security features on this repo
expected_behavior: enumerates secret scanning, push protection, dependabot alerts/updates, and private vulnerability reporting with the exact gh api/gh commands
distinguishes what is free on public repos vs needs GHAS/paid on private ones
verifies each toggle after applying instead of assuming success
